task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard is created and named exactly 'Cost Efficiency Ratios'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Cost Efficiency Ratios' is visible as the dashboard title in the Graphite web interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Network per CPU' graph is created with the correct divideSeries target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Network per CPU' is visible. The target input field or metric tree shows the exact function: divideSeries(servers.ec2_instance_1.network.bytes_in, servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Fleet CPU Aggregate' graph is created with the correct alias and sumSeries target, without using wildcards.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Fleet CPU Aggregate' is visible. The target input field shows: alias(sumSeries(servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization, servers.ec2_instance_2.cpu.utilization, servers.ec2_instance_3.cpu.cloudwatch_utilization), 'Total Fleet CPU'). No '*' wildcards should be present in this target.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Disk IO per Request' graph is created with the correct divideSeries target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Disk IO per Request' is visible. The target input field shows the exact function: divideSeries(servers.ec2_instance_1.disk.write_bytes, servers.load_balancer.requests.count).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Hourly CPU Summary' graph is created with the correct summarize target.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Hourly CPU Summary' is visible. The target input field shows the exact function: summarize(servers.ec2_instance_2.cpu.utilization, '1h', 'avg').

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Dashboard saved' confirmation message is visible, the save dialog is being confirmed, or the dashboard is shown in the saved dashboards list in the Graphite Finder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Graphite web GUI to create the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Graphite web interface (browser window) being interacted with, rather than terminal commands (like curl) or a script being written to generate the dashboard.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply write a JSON dashboard definition in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dashboard creation process should be visible within the Graphite UI (adding graphs, editing targets), not inside a text editor like nano, vim, or gedit editing a .json file.

integrity.description: Verify the graphs are actually rendering within the Graphite application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should display axes, legends, or data lines typical of the Graphite renderer, proving the targets were actually applied in the application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected dashboard name is exactly 'Cost Efficiency Ratios'. The four required graph titles are 'Network per CPU', 'Fleet CPU Aggregate', 'Disk IO per Request', and 'Hourly CPU Summary'. The graphs must use the exact transformation functions specified in the task description (divideSeries, alias(sumSeries(...)), divideSeries, and summarize respectively). The sumSeries target must explicitly list the three instances and not use wildcards.
